columns.extraction_plan: The add-on treatment is Darolutamide. This is explicitly stated in the abstract on page 1: "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." This indicates that darolutamide (or placebo) is added to the standard backbone of ADT and docetaxel. The "Trial Design" section on page 2 further confirms this: "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel."

columns.extraction_plan: From Table 3, under "All Patients" and "Darolutamide (n = 652)", sum the counts and percentages for "Grade 3", "Grade 4", and "Grade 5 (death)". columns.extraction_plan: This column refers to the overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence in randomized controlled trials. This type of assessment (e.g., risk of bias assessment) is typically performed by external reviewers in systematic reviews or meta-analyses, not usually reported within a primary clinical trial publication itself. The ARASENS trial report does not contain an overall risk judgment of its own individual criteria of evidence.

columns.extraction_plan: The control arm is explicitly stated in the abstract and methods section. On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section of the abstract, it states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' On page 2, in the 'Trial Design' section under 'METHODS', it states: 'Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel.'

On page 8, refer to Table 2, "Secondary Efficacy End Points for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS". Locate the column header "All Patients - Placebo (n = 654)". The number of participants in the control arm is 654.

The Discussion section on page 6 (Chunk 9) explicitly confirms, "The results of this post hoc analysis of the ARASENS trial, in which all patients received docetaxel, show that darolutamide in combination with ADT and docetaxel increases OS in patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." This indicates that 100% of patients in the treatment arm received docetaxel. The exact count for the treatment arm is not provided in the given chunks.

This indicates that 100% of patients in the control arm received docetaxel. The exact count for the control arm is not provided in the given chunks.

The number and percentage of participants with a Performance Status (PS) score of 0 in the Darolutamide treatment arm can be derived by summing the counts from the "High Volume" and "Low Volume" subgroups in Table 1. From Table 1, page 5, under "ECOG PS, No. (%)", for "0": Darolutamide (n = 497) High Volume: 343 (69.0%), Darolutamide (n = 154) Low Volume: 123 (79.9%). Summing these gives: 343 + 123 = 466 patients. The total number of Darolutamide patients in these subgroups is 497 + 154 = 651. The percentage is (466 / 651) * 100 = 71.6%. Therefore, the value is 466 (71.6%).

The number and percentage of participants with a Performance Status (PS) score of 0 in the Placebo control arm can be derived by summing the counts from the "High Volume" and "Low Volume" subgroups in Table 1. From Table 1, page 5, under "ECOG PS, No. (%)", for "0": Placebo (n = 508) High Volume: 349 (68.7%), Placebo (n = 146) Low Volume: 113 (77.4%). Summing these gives: 349 + 113 = 462 patients. The total number of Placebo patients in these subgroups is 508 + 146 = 654. The percentage is (462 / 654) * 100 = 70.6%. Therefore, the value is 462 (70.6%).

Table 1, page 5, reports ECOG PS scores as "0" or "1". No patients are reported with PS 2. Therefore, "PS 1-2" is interpreted as "PS 1". The number and percentage of participants with a Performance Status (PS) score of 1 in the Darolutamide treatment arm can be derived by summing the counts from the "High Volume" and "Low Volume" subgroups. From Table 1, page 5, under "ECOG PS, No. (%)", for "1": Darolutamide (n = 497) High Volume: 154 (31.0%), Darolutamide (n = 154) Low Volume: 31 (20.1%). Summing these gives: 154 + 31 = 185 patients. The total number of Darolutamide patients in these subgroups is 497 + 154 = 651. The percentage is (185 / 651) * 100 = 28.4%. Therefore, the value is 185 (28.4%).

Table 1, page 5, reports ECOG PS scores as "0" or "1". No patients are reported with PS 2. Therefore, "PS 1-2" is interpreted as "PS 1". From Table 1, page 5, under "ECOG PS, No. (%)", for "1": Placebo (n = 508) High Volume: 157 (30.9%), Placebo (n = 146) Low Volume: 33 (22.6%). Summing these gives: 157 + 33 = 190 patients. The total number of Placebo patients in these subgroups is 508 + 146 = 654. The percentage is (190 / 654) * 100 = 29.0%. Therefore, the value is 190 (29.0%).

columns.extraction_plan: The primary endpoint is identified as Overall Survival (OS). This is inferred from the 'RESULTS' section of the abstract, which prominently reports 'Darolutamide increased overall survival (OS) versus placebo...' as the main efficacy outcome, and subsequently mentions 'clinically relevant secondary end points', thereby implying OS is the primary endpoint.

Chunk 4 (Table 2) reports 'Time to worsening disease-related symptoms'. This measures specific physical symptoms and does not align with the definition of an 'overall Quality of Life Scale' which assesses general well-being and satisfaction with health status (a measure of the participants' overall well-being and satisfaction with their health status, as assessed through a standardized questionnaire or survey, reflecting their perceived quality of life during the clinical trial?). No other provided chunk reports a measure that fits the definition of an overall Quality of Life Scale. Therefore, an overall Quality of Life Scale is not reported.

The provided chunk (Chunk 8, pages 1-4) does not contain information explicitly stating that the clinical trial reports results on the quality of life of participants, nor does it contain the specific quotes or assessment tools (e.g., Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate) mentioned in the original plan. Therefore, based solely on the provided chunk, it is 'Not reported'.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous
columns.extraction_plan: Yes. The text on page 2 states: "The presence of metastatic disease at initial diagnosis (de novo or synchronous disease) is associated with poorer outcomes..." and "In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85)... metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." This indicates reporting for the "de novo" (synchronous) group.
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous
columns.extraction_plan: Yes. The text on page 2 states: "The presence of metastatic disease at initial diagnosis (de novo or synchronous disease) is associated with poorer outcomes compared with developing metastases after local therapy (recurrent or metachronous disease)." and "In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with ... recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." This indicates reporting for the "recurrent" (metachronous) group.
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume
columns.extraction_plan: Yes. The "PURPOSE" section on page 1 states: "We examined efﬁcacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk." The "METHODS" section on page 2 defines: "High-volume disease was deﬁned as visceral metastases and/or four or more bone metastases with one or more beyond the vertebral column and pelvis." The "RESULTS" section on page 1 reports: "Dar-olutamide increased overall survival (OS) versus placebo in patients with high-volume (hazard ratio [HR], 0.69; 95%CI, 0.57 to 0.82)..."
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume
columns.extraction_plan: Yes. The "PURPOSE" section on page 1 states: "We examined efﬁcacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk." The "METHODS" section on page 2 states: "Low-volume and low-risk disease were deﬁned as not meeting the" (criteria for high-volume/high-risk). The "RESULTS" section on page 1 reports: "...and in the smaller low-volume subgroup, the results were also suggestive of survival beneﬁt (HR, 0.68; 95% CI, 0.41to 1.13)."
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume

columns.extraction_plan: The secondary endpoints are explicitly mentioned in the abstract on page 1. They are: Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer and Time to initiation of subsequent systemic antineoplastic therapy (or subsequent systemic antineoplastic therapy).

The requested endpoint 'Time To Progression of Serum Androgen (TTPSA)' is not found in the provided Table 2 or its associated text. The table contains 'Time to CRPC', which is a different endpoint. Therefore, the value is 'Not reported'.

The requested endpoint 'Time to Progressive Serum Androgen (TTPSA)' is not found in the provided Table 2 or its associated text. The table contains 'Time to CRPC', which is a different endpoint. Therefore, the value is 'Not reported'.

The total number of participants is 1,305. This value can be found in the "RESULTS" section of the abstract on page 1, which states: "Of 1,305 patients, 1,005 (77%) had high-volume disease and 912 (70%) had high-risk disease."

For the Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT arm, sum the 'n' values from the 'High Volume - Darolutamide (n = 497)' column header and the 'Low Volume - Darolutamide (n = 154)' column header in Table 1. For the Placebo + docetaxel + ADT arm, sum the 'n' values from the 'High Volume - Placebo (n = 508)' column header and the 'Low Volume - Placebo (n = 146)' column header in Table 1.

Extract the regimen for the experimental arm from the "PURPOSE" section of the abstract on page 1: "darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." The darolutamide arm is the experimental arm. Further confirmation is found in the "Trial Design" section on page 2: "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel."

The experimental treatment arm is reported in the abstract and methods section. Extract the name of the arm from the "METHODS" section on page 1, which states: "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel." The experimental arm is the one receiving darolutamide.

The treatment regimen consists of three classes of therapy. These are explicitly mentioned in the "INTRODUCTION" section on page 1 (Chunk 8) and the "abstract" on page 1 (Chunk 8). The combination of these three is also referred to as "triplet therapy" in the "Relevance" section on page 2 (Chunk 8).
The trial name "ARASENS" is explicitly stated in the title of the paper and throughout the text.

The type of therapy for the intervention arm is "Darolutamide plus androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) and docetaxel". This is explicitly stated in the title of the article and in the "METHODS" section of the abstract.

columns.column_name: Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: From Table 1, on page 5, locate the column 'High Volume - Darolutamide (n = 497)'. The number of patients is 497. To find the total number of patients in the Darolutamide arm, sum the 'n' values from 'High Volume - Darolutamide (n = 497)' and 'Low Volume - Darolutamide (n = 154)' from Table 1 (497 + 154 = 651). Calculate the percentage as (497 / 651) * 100.
columns.column_name_raw: Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treatment

Calculate the percentage as (508 / 654) * 100.
columns.column_name_raw: Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Control
